notes: Acts as the unique identifier for this specific AI detection (bounding box).
Camtrap DP handles taxonomic hierarchy internally or via external taxonomic providers. You can safely drop this or map it to observationTags.
The assigned class. If this is a common name rather than scientific, it could technically go in observationComments, but scientificName is standard for taxon labels.
External taxonomy ID. Could be appended to observationComments if strictly needed.
Map directly. Represents the AI's confidence score.
Internal algorithm logic. Safely ignored.
Captures the AI model name (e.g., "fastai-species"). You should also hardcode classificationMethod = "machine".
The unique ID of the image. Must exist in the Media table to be referenced by the Observation table.
Top-left X coordinate.
Requires transformation: bboxWidth = x2 - x1.
Top-left Y coordinate.
Requires transformation: bboxHeight = y2 - y1.
Camtrap DP links to the full original image, not the crop.
Acts as the human-readable site code (e.g., "DK3").
Redundant; the year will be extracted from the timestamp.
Project-level metadata, doesn't belong in these core tables.
Project-level metadata.
Requires parsing. POINT(X Y) extracts to longitude = X, latitude = Y.
The raw name of the image file.
The primary relational key tying all three tables together.
Use the URL as the file path if the images are hosted remotely (standard practice).
Requires transformation: Convert Unix epoch (1747708799) to ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2025-05-20T02:39:59Z).
